steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The second sentence grants a limitation: Beaumont's painting doesn't technically represent a fourth dimension because humans see only in three. The blank sentence then pivots to say the multi-perspective approach still offers a glimpse of that 4D world. The transition must signal a concession — "yes, that's true, but here's the positive takeaway."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — For example,: Introduces an illustration of the preceding idea. The blank sentence doesn't exemplify the limitation — it moves past it. ✗
  • Choice B — In other words,: Signals a restatement. The blank sentence introduces new information (the multi-perspective glimpse) rather than restating what came before. ✗
  • Choice C — Moreover,: Signals an additive elaboration in the same direction. The blank sentence actually turns against the limitation with a positive counter — "moreover" doesn't capture that pivot. ✗
  • Choice D — That said,: Signals "despite what was just acknowledged" — the classic concession pivot. Acknowledges the 3D limitation, then introduces the positive consequence of Beaumont's multi-perspective technique. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
